notes_for_human_reviewer: OVER-CORRECTION FAILURE MODE. The learner has read the chapter and tried to work the model backward — but went one step too far, diagnosing the trouble as initiation-incomplete rather than planning-incomplete. The scenario does not support 'unclear sponsorship' or 'unclear business case' — those words are not in the scenario. The learner has imported a generic diagnosis. The transition naming (initiation-to-planning) is wrong; the actual failure is planning-to-execution. The recommendation to pause the build for three weeks and re-do initiation is the wrong intervention. Methodology angle ('switch to pure waterfall') is reactive and wrong. Tests whether the grader catches a confidently-wrong diagnosis vs a humble correct-but-shallow one. Phase_diagnosis tolerance widened to 2 because reasonable graders could score 1-3 here — the answer is wrong but the structure of the thinking matches the chapter.
